_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder

title: Community Solution: WIRE
subtitle: work together, inspire, respond, empower!
overview: In this session, students learn how communities solve problems together using the WIRE model — Work Together, Inspire Ideas, Respond to Needs, and Empower People. Students explore teamwork, empathy, communication, and innovation while designing solutions for real community challenges.

images._note: Add photos from teamwork activities, community surveys, brainstorming sessions, and solution presentations.

gallery.caption: Communities grow stronger when people work together
Creative thinking helps solve community challenges
Empathy and teamwork create meaningful impact

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Poster or awareness campaign activity
gallery.caption: Awareness campaigns inspire positive action

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Cleanliness or social service activity
gallery.caption: Small actions can create big community change

keyTopics: W — Work Together: Teamwork builds stronger communities
I — Inspire Ideas: Creativity leads to innovation
R — Respond to Needs: Understand and solve real problems
E — Empower People: Help others grow and participate
Social Innovation — Creating solutions for society

youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how innovative ideas can improve society and communities.

activity.title: Design a Community Solution
steps: Identify one problem in your school or community
Discuss how the problem affects people
Brainstorm creative solutions using the WIRE model
Explain how your solution helps the community
Present your idea to the class

quiz.q: What does the 'W' in WIRE stand for?
quiz.explanation: W stands for Work Together because teamwork helps communities solve problems.

quiz.q: What does the 'I' in WIRE stand for?
quiz.explanation: I stands for Inspire Ideas — creativity and innovation help communities grow.

quiz.q: Why is teamwork important in communities?
options: One person can solve every problem
quiz.explanation: Working together allows people to combine skills and ideas for better solutions.
Empathy means understanding and caring about other people's feelings and needs.

quiz.q: Which is an example of a community problem?
quiz.explanation: Garbage pollution is a real community problem that needs solutions.

quiz.q: What does the 'R' in WIRE stand for?
quiz.explanation: R means Respond to Needs by understanding and solving real problems.

quiz.q: What does the 'E' in WIRE stand for?
quiz.explanation: E stands for Empower People by helping and encouraging others.
Social innovation focuses on solving problems that help people and communities.

quiz.q: Why should students participate in community activities?
quiz.explanation: Students can become changemakers by helping improve their communities.

quiz.q: Which skill helps students share ideas clearly?
quiz.explanation: Communication helps students explain and present their ideas effectively.
